itineraries.title: SAINT BARTHÉLEMY: TURQUOISE LAGOONS, SWEDISH HERITAGE AND WILD BEACHES
itineraries.subtitle: From Gustavia's free port to Colombier's isolated coves, explore the hidden treasures of a preserved island balancing discreet luxury and protected nature.
itineraries.notes: Circuit exploring bays and villages of Saint Barthélemy: Gustavia and its Swedish heritage, paradise beaches of St-Jean and Saline, coastal hikes to Colombier, Grand Cul-de-Sac lagoon for water sports.

seo.h1_title: Saint Barthélemy Circuit: Paradise Beaches, Swedish Heritage and Turquoise Lagoons
seo.intro_paragraph: Explore Saint Barthélemy, a French jewel of the Lesser Antilles spanning 25 km² blending discreet luxury and preserved nature. From Gustavia, the legendary free port with three 18th-century Swedish forts, to the wild coves of Colombier and Saline accessible only on foot, this circuit reveals the hidden treasures of a unique island. Discover St-Jean airport and its spectacular 646-meter runway ranked 3rd most dangerous in the world, hike to isolated beaches bordered by crystal-clear waters, and dive in the turquoise lagoons of Grand Cul-de-Sac where sea turtles and tropical fish thrive.
seo.seo_hook: Discover authentic Saint Barthélemy: a circuit combining coastal hikes, deserted beaches, unique Swedish heritage and water sports in protected lagoons.
featured_list: Gustavia: three Swedish forts (Gustav, Karl, Oscar) overlooking the free port established in 1785
Colombier Cove: 30-minute hike from Petite Anse to isolated beach in the nature reserve
St-Jean: 646-meter landing strip ranked 3rd most dangerous airport in the world
Grand Cul-de-Sac: shallow lagoon protected by reef, renowned kitesurf and windsurf spot
Saline: wild beach accessible by 10-minute walk through dunes, nudism tolerated
Shell Beach: crushed shells covering the beach, 5 minutes walk from downtown Gustavia

places_taglines.BL::gustavia: Swedish free port with three historic forts and preserved colonial architecture
places_taglines.BL::st-jean: Legendary beach under the spectacular 646-meter landing strip
places_taglines.BL::colombier: Isolated gem accessible only by foot or boat - marine nature reserve
places_taglines.BL::cul-de-sac: Turquoise lagoon kitesurfing paradise with shallow waters inhabited by turtles

practical_context.vehicle_type: Compact rental car or mini-jeep recommended - narrow winding roads
practical_context.group_type: Couples seeking discreet luxury, families with children, solo travelers who love beaches and hiking
practical_context.loop_type: Loop circuit around the island - daily return to Gustavia or St-Jean
highlights: St-Jean landing strip 646m - 3rd most dangerous airport in the world, daily spectacle guaranteed
Colombier beach accessible only by 30-min foot or boat - protected marine nature reserve
Gustavia free port - unique Swedish heritage 1784-1878 with three dominant historic forts
Grand Cul-de-Sac lagoon - shallow turquoise waters, renowned kitesurf and windsurf spot with sea turtles
